name: Water Point Data Exchange (WPdx-Basic)

columns.name: status_clean
columns.description: Categorized version of the #status parameter. Based on terms from the #status entry, status_clean includes 5 categories: Fully functional, Functional but needs repair, Non functional and needs repair, Non functional due to dry season, Abandoned and Other. These categories will continue to evolve and will be refined in future updates.

columns.name: #notes
columns.description: This field can be used to incorporate any additional information not already part ofthe WPDx standard that is useful to the data provider.

columns.name: #photo_lnk
columns.description: Provide the URL of a photograph of the water system. Multiple URLs can beincluded, with each URL separated by a semi-colon (;).

columns.name: #orig_lnk
columns.description: Provide the public link to the data record for a specific water point or full data set,including any non-standard compliant data.

columns.name: facility_type
columns.description: Categorized facility type based on JMP definitions

columns.name: water_tech_clean
columns.description: Cleaned/categorized version of raw #water_tech entries

columns.name: water_source_clean
columns.description: Cleaned/categorized version of raw #water_source entries

columns.name: clean_adm4
columns.description: Administrative division 4 for water point record based on GPS and GADM or HDX dataset boundaries.

columns.name: #lat_deg
columns.description: Provide the decimal value of the latitude in WGS 1984

columns.name: #lon_deg
columns.description: Provide the decimal value of the longitude in WGS 1984

columns.name: #report_date
columns.description: Provide the date that the data was collected

columns.name: #status_id
columns.description: Identify if any water is available on the day of the visit, recognizing that itmay be alimited flow

columns.name: #water_source
columns.description: Describe the water source (e.g. shallow well, spring, borehole, river, pond, etc.)

columns.name: #water_tech
columns.description: Describe the system being used to transport the water from the source to the point of collection (e.g. Handpump (include manufacturer, i.e. Afridev, India Mark II, Malda, etc.), Kiosk, Tapstand, etc.).

columns.name: #country_name
columns.description: Country where water point is located

columns.name: #adm1
columns.description: Provide the name of the primary administrative division. The correct unit can befound at http://www.statoids.com. This corresponds to “First Order” and “First Level”administrative units at http://Geonames.organd http://www.gadm.orgrespectively

columns.name: #adm2
columns.description: Provide the name of the secondary administrative division. The correct unit can befound at http://www.statoids.com. This corresponds to “Second Order” and “Second Level”administrative units at http://Geonames.organd http://www.gadm.orgrespectively

columns.name: #adm3
columns.description: Provide the name of the tertiaryadministrative division. The correct unit can befound at http://www.statoids.com. This corresponds to “ThirdOrder” and “ThirdLevel”administrative units at http://Geonames.organd http://www.gadm.orgrespectively

columns.name: #install_year
columns.description: Provide the 4-digit installation year

columns.name: #installer
columns.description: : Provide the name of the entity or entities that installed the water system. Thisshould be the entities that completed or weredirectly responsible for the construction,ratherthan a donor or other involved stakeholder

columns.name: #rehab_year
columns.description: Provide the 4-digit year when the most recent major rehabilitation (not just regularmaintenance)occurred

columns.name: #rehabilitator
columns.description: Provide the name of the entity or entities that completed the most recentrehabilitation of the water system. Thisshould be the entities that complete or were directlyresponsible for the construction, ratherthan a donor or other involved stakeholder

columns.name: #management
columns.description: Select the classification of the entity that directly manages the water point.

columns.name: #pay
columns.description: Provide the payment amount and basis (e.g. monthly, per jerry can, when broken,etc.). If no amount is provided, the basis can be provided alone. An amount without a paymentbasis cannot be included

columns.name: #status
columns.description: Provide a status ofthe physical/mechanical condition of thewater point

columns.name: #fecal_coliform_value
columns.description: Results of e. coli or thermotolerant coliform water quality test froma 100ml watersample collected directly from water point.Total coliform should not be included. Ifthermotolerant, must be noted in themetadata. Value should represent themost probable number or colony forming units in 100ml.

columns.name: #fecal_coliform_presence
columns.description: Results of e. coli or thermotolerant coliform water quality test froma 100ml watersample collected directly from water point.Total coliform should not be included. Ifthermotolerant, must be noted in themetadata. Value should represent presence or absence.

columns.name: #subjective_quality
columns.description: Information regarding the perceived quality of the water including taste,appearance, and/or odor

columns.name: #activity_id
columns.description: Provide the Unique ID for the specific water point infrastructure, as reported bydata collector. This could be a physical ID on the water point or an internal system ID.

columns.name: #scheme_id
columns.description: The identifier for asmall piped scheme that connects multiple individual waterpoints.

columns.name: #subjective_quality_clean
columns.description: Cleaned version of the #subjective_quality parameter

columns.name: #pay_clean
columns.description: Cleaned version of the #pay parameter.

columns.name: dataset_title
columns.description: Name of dataset associated with upload

columns.name: Countries
columns.description: This column was automatically created in order to record in what polygon from the dataset 'Countries' (7zzf-xi3x) the point in column 'geocoded_column' is located.  This enables the creation of region maps (choropleths) in the visualization canvas and data lens.

columns.name: #source
columns.description: Provide the name of the organization collecting the data record

columns.name: row_id
columns.description: Row number

description: The Water Point Data Exchange (WPdx) is the global platform for sharing water point data. The WPdx Data Standard was designed by a wide range of stakeholders from across sectors and around the world. The core attributes included in the standard are already being collected by governments, researchers, and organizations around the world. To read more about the standard or to contribute water point data visit www.waterpointdata.org.

name: Water Point Data Exchange - Plus (WPdx+)

columns.name: usage_capacity
columns.description:
Recommended maximum users per water point, extended from Sphere Guidelines (https://spherestandards.org/wp-content/uploads/Sphere-Handbook-2018-EN.pdf) including:

    250 people per tap [tapstand, kiosk, rainwater catchment]
    500 people per hand pump [all hand pumps]
    400 people per open hand well [rope and bucket]
    1000 people per mechanized well

columns.name: pressure_score
columns.description: The pressure score (0-100%) is calculated based on the ratio of the number of people assigned to that water point over the theoretical maximum population which can be served based on the technology. If a point is serving less than the recommended maximum, the pressure score will be less than 100% (i.e., 250/500 = 0.5). If a point is serving more than the recommended maximum, the pressure score will be over 100% (i.e., 750/500 = 150%).

columns.name: local_population_1km
columns.description: The number of people living within a 1km radius of the water point.

columns.name: #pop_who_would_gain_access
columns.description: Number of people who would gain new access to a functional point if a specific point was made functional again. This number may be the same or less that the "Rehab Priority" population, as some people living within 1km of a broken point may have already had access to a different functional point. Rehab priority calculates all potential people who could use the repaired point, while pop who would gain access is focused only on the population that did not have access to any functional point.

columns.name: updated_timestamp
columns.description: Date updated.

columns.name: staleness_score
columns.description:
The staleness score represents the depreciation of the point's relevance - older updates are less valuable than fresh data. The main parameter that controls this score is the 'target value' - for example, in the current state we define that a 20-year-old report is worth only 10% in comparison to a 'day-old' report. All other reports are then given values based on their report age using a geometric decay model.
These value assignments are very qualitative and are only meant to give a rough estimate of data 'freshness’.

columns.name: water_point_population
columns.description: Number of people currently or potentially served by a specific water point. For working water points, this number represents likely current users. For non-functional water points, this number represents users who would gain access if the point was functional.

columns.name: #rehabilitator
columns.description: Name of the entity or entities that completed the most recent rehabilitation of the water system. This should be the entities that complete or were directly responsible for the construction, rather than a donor or other involved stakeholder.

columns.name: rehab_priority
columns.description: Ranked priority for rehabilitation based on the number of users who would gain access if the water point was returned to a functional state.

columns.name: water_point_history
columns.description: Summary of previous water point records with same WPdx_id. Only the most recent water point record is displayed in the row.

columns.name: #distance_to_town
columns.description: Distance in kilometers to the nearest town based on calculations with OpenStreetMap data.

columns.name: #distance_to_tertiary_road
columns.description: Distance in kilometers to the third-closest road based on calculations with OpenStreetMap data.

columns.name: #data_lnk
columns.description: The Water Point Data Exchange catalog link for relevant dataset.

columns.name: #photo_lnk
columns.description: URL of a photograph of the water system. Multiple URLs may be included, with each URL separated by a semi-colon (;).

columns.name: #orig_lnk
columns.description: Public link to the data record for a specific water point or full data set, including any non-standard compliant data.

columns.name: #wpdx_id
columns.description: The Water Point Data Exchange ID. Assigned based on location and used to match records from water points over from different organizations and report dates.

columns.name: #scheme_id
columns.description: The unique ID for a small piped scheme that connects multiple individual water points.

columns.name: #activity_id
columns.description: Unique ID for the specific water point infrastructure, as reported by data collector. This could be a physical ID on the water point or an internal system ID.

columns.name: #subjective_quality
columns.description: Information regarding the perceived quality of the water including taste, appearance, and/or odor.

columns.name: #fecal_coliform_value
columns.description: Results of e. coli or thermotolerant coliform water quality test from a 100ml water sample collected directly from water point. Total coliform should not be included. If thermotolerant, must be noted in the metadata. Value should represent the most probable number or colony forming units in 100ml.

columns.name: #pay
columns.description: Payment amount and basis (e.g. monthly, per jerry can, when broken, etc.). If no amount is provided, the basis can be provided alone. An amount without a payment basis cannot be included.

columns.name: #status_clean
columns.description: Categorized version of the #status parameter. Based on terms from the #status entry, status_clean includes 5 categories: Fully functional, Functional but needs repair, Non functional and needs repair, Non functional due to dry season, Abandoned and Other. These categories will continue to evolve and will be refined in future updates.

columns.name: #management_clean
columns.description: Cleaned and categorized version of the management structure data. Categories include: Community management, Direct government operation, School Management, Private Person, Health Care Facility, Other Institutional Management, Private Operator

columns.name: New Georeferenced Column
columns.description: For spatial visualizations in socrata

columns.name: lat_lon_deg
columns.description: The geographic coordinates where the water point is located.

columns.name: public_data_source
columns.description: Public link to the source file.

columns.name: created_timestamp
columns.description: Timestamp of the date when the data was created

columns.name: #pay_clean
columns.description: Cleaned version of #pay parameter, focusing only on basis and not amount. Options include: No payment, Fees collected – basis unknown, Fees collected – point of collection, Fees collected – metered, Fees collected – monthly, Fees collected – seasonally, Fees collected – upon breakdown

columns.name: #subjective_quality_clean
columns.description: Cleaned version of the #subjective_quality parameter. Options include: Acceptable quality, Unacceptable quality, Bad taste, Bad color, Bad smell

columns.name: is_duplicate
columns.description: A check for exact duplicates. All columns must have the same entries to be marked as a duplicate here.

columns.name: dataset_title
columns.description: Name of dataset associated with the WPdx upload

columns.name: Countries
columns.description: This column was automatically created in order to record in what polygon from the dataset 'Countries' (7zzf-xi3x) the point in column 'new_georeferenced_column_' is located.  This enables the creation of region maps (choropleths) in the visualization canvas and data lens.

columns.name: #adm1
columns.description: Provide the name of the primary administrative division. The correct unit can be found at http://www.statoids.com. This corresponds to “First Order” and “First Level” administrative units at http://Geonames.org and http://www.gadm.org respectively.

columns.name: #adm2
columns.description: Provide the name of the secondary administrative division. The correct unit can be found at http://www.statoids.com. This corresponds to “Second Order” and “Second Level” administrative units at http://Geonames.org and http://www.gadm.org respectively.

columns.name: #adm3
columns.description: Provide the name of the tertiary administrative division. The correct unit can be found at http://www.statoids.com. This corresponds to “Third Order” and “Third Level” administrative units at http://Geonames.org and http://www.gadm.org respectively.

columns.name: row_id
columns.description: Row number

columns.name: #source
columns.description: Provide the name of the organization collecting the data record.

columns.name: #lat_deg
columns.description: Provide the decimal value of the latitude in WGS 1984. At least four decimals (but more are encouraged) should be included. North and East should be noted as positive numbers.

columns.name: #lon_deg
columns.description: Provide the decimal value of the longitude in WGS 1984. At least four decimals (but more are encouraged) should be included. North and East should be noted as positive numbers.

columns.name: #report_date
columns.description: Provide the date that the data was collected on using ISO 8601. Time and time zone designator are optional.

columns.name: #status_id
columns.description: Identify if any water is available on the day of the visit, recognizing that it may be a limited flow.

columns.name: #water_source_clean
columns.description: Cleaned version of the water source data. Includes cleaned and detailed data (Protected vs. unprotected wells, etc)

columns.name: #water_source_category
columns.description: Categorized version of the water source data. Categories include Well, Spring, Rainwater Harvesting, Piped, Sand/subsurface dam, Delivered Water

columns.name: #water_tech_clean
columns.description: Cleaned version of the water point technology data. Includes cleaned and detailed data (type of handpump, etc.)

columns.name: #water_tech_category
columns.description: Categorized version of the water point technology data. Categories include: Hand pump, Mechanized pump, Tapstand, Rope and Bucket

columns.name: #facility_type
columns.description: Categorized facility type based on JMP definitions: https://washdata.org/monitoring/methods/facility-types

columns.name: #clean_country_name
columns.description: Cleaned version of the country name based on provided GPS coordinates.

columns.name: #clean_adm4
columns.description: Adm4 based on provided GPS coordinates and GADM or HDX/OCHA boundaries

columns.name: #install_year
columns.description: Provide the 4-digit installation year (e.g. 1994).

columns.name: #rehab_year
columns.description: 4-digit year when the most recent major rehabilitation (not just regular maintenance) occurred (e.g. 1994).

columns.name: #clean_adm1
columns.description: Cleaned version of the Primary Administrative Division data based on provided GPS coordinates and GADM boundaries.

columns.name: #clean_adm2
columns.description: Cleaned version of the Secondary Administrative Division data  based on provided GPS coordinates and GADM boundaries.

columns.name: #clean_adm3
columns.description: Cleaned version of the Tertiary Administrative Division data  based on provided GPS coordinates and GADM boundaries.

columns.name: converted
columns.description: List of all fields converted by WPDx from original data source.

columns.name: count
columns.description: The number of records of this type.

columns.name: #country_id
columns.description: The ISO two letter country classification code, selected from a list of all ISO country codes.

columns.name: crucialness_score
columns.description: The crucialness score (0-100%) is the ratio of likely current users to the total local population within a 1km radius of the water point. Crucialness provides a measure of water system redundancy. For non-functional water points, the crucialness score shows how important the water point would be if it were to be rehabilitated.

columns.name: #distance_to_city
columns.description: Distance in kilometers to the nearest city based on calculations with OpenStreetMap data.

columns.name: #distance_to_primary_road
columns.description: Distance in kilometers to the nearest road based on calculations with OpenStreetMap data.

columns.name: #distance_to_secondary_road
columns.description: Distance in kilometers to the second-closest road based on calculations with OpenStreetMap data.

columns.name: #fecal_coliform_presence
columns.description: Results of e. coli or thermotolerant coliform water quality test from a 100ml water sample collected directly from water point. Total coliform should not be included. If thermotolerant, must be noted in the metadata. Value should represent presence or absence.

columns.name: #installer
columns.description: Name of the entity or entities that installed the water system. This should be the entities that completed or were directly responsible for the construction, rather than a donor or other involved stakeholder.

columns.name: location_id
columns.description: A counting tool for each new location that appears in a dataset

columns.name: #notes
columns.description: This field can be used to incorporate any additional information not already part of the WPDx standard that is useful to the data provider. Exact data included is unique per data set and can be found in the WPDx Catalog: https://catalog.waterpointdata.org/datasets/

columns.name: #management
columns.description: Select the classification of the entity that directly manages the water point. Category suggestions include: “Direct Government Operation”, “Private Operator/Delegated Management”, “Community Management”, “School”, “Health Care Facility”, “Religious Institution”, “Other Institutional Management”, “Other.”

columns.name: #status
columns.description: Status of the physical/mechanical condition of the water point.

columns.name: #water_tech
columns.description: Describe the system being used to transport the water from the source to the point of collection (e.g. Handpump (include manufacturer, i.e. Afridev, India Mark II, Malda, etc.), Kiosk, Tapstand, etc.).

columns.name: #water_source
columns.description: Describe the water source (e.g. shallow well, spring, borehole, river, pond, etc.).

columns.name: #country_name
columns.description: Provide the name of the country where water point is located.

columns.name: #clean_country_id
columns.description: Cleaned version of the country ID data.

columns.name: cluster_size
columns.description: The number of times a location appears in the dataset.

columns.name: latest_record
columns.description: True/False value indicating if this data is the latest for each water point.

columns.name: days_since_report
columns.description: Number of days which have passed since the report date. Updated on approx a weekly basis when the analyses are re-run to incorporate new data uploads.

columns.name: is_urban
columns.description: Is in an urban area as defined by EU Global Human Settlement Database (https://ghsl.jrc.ec.europa.eu/ghs_stat_ucdb2015mt_r2019a.php)
